7.reason: [Using 'gets' is unsafe and deprecated; it can cause buffer overflow. Moreover, some compilers may not support it, leading to compilation errors or warnings treated as errors.]

14.reason: [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts offsets from 'A', which gives same result as direct subtraction but is unnecessarily complex and error-prone. However, the real issue is that when s[i+1]==s[i-1], the loop runs negative times, but condition above ensures s[i+1]>=s[i-1]. Still, if equal, no expansion should occur per problem statement ("greater than"), but condition allows equality. This violates spec. But compilation error likely due to 'gets'.]

Current logic subtracts offsets from 'A', which gives same result as direct subtraction but is unnecessarily complex and error-prone. However, the real issue is that when s[i+1]==s[i-1], the loop runs negative times, but condition above ensures s[i+1]>=s[i-1]. Still, if equal, no expansion should occur per problem statement ("greater than"), but condition allows equality. This violates spec. But compilation error likely due to 'gets'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts base offsets unnecessarily and skips the last character.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]); current logic subtracts base twice unnecessarily]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts offsets unnecessarily and may skip valid characters. Also, condition allows s[i+1]==s[i-1], which violates requirement that the latter must be greater.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect loop bound: should be j <= (s[i+1] - s[i-1] - 1) or simply j < (s[i+1] - s[i-1]). Current logic subtracts offsets unnecessarily and skips the correct number of characters.]

37.reason: [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not all three type checks. So if uppercase or digit conditions fail but lowercase also fails, it prints '-', but if uppercase succeeds, it still checks digit/lowercase and may print extra '-' due to dangling else. Also, missing bounds check for i==0 or i==strlen(s)-1 leading to out-of-bounds access.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not all three type checks. So if uppercase or digit conditions fail but lowercase also fails, it prints '-', but if uppercase succeeds, it still checks digit/lowercase and may print extra '-' due to dangling else. Also, missing bounds check for i==0 or i==strlen(s)-1 leading to out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not the entire group; so if '-' is between uppercase or digits but doesn't meet condition, it won't print '-', leading to missing output]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not all three type checks. So if the '-' is between uppercase or digits but doesn't satisfy their conditions, it falls through and prints '-' even if one of the earlier cases might have been intended to handle it. This causes incorrect output for mixed-type or invalid ranges. Also, missing bounds check for i==0 or i==strlen(s)-1 leads to out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (lowercase), not all three type checks. So if uppercase or digit conditions fail but lowercase also fails, it prints '-', but if uppercase succeeds and lowercase fails, it still prints '-' incorrectly. Logic structure is flawed.]
